name: MITRAL VALVE PROLAPSE
definition: MVP = systolic bowing of mitral leaflet > 2 mm beyond annular plane into atrium

demographics.Prevalence: 2-6% of general population; 5-20% of young women; ? autosomal dominant
demographics.Path: myxomatous proliferation of middle layer of MV leaflets, surface fibrosis of MV
demographics.Location: middle scallop of posterior leaflet (P2 segment)

dominant_finding: abrupt midsystolic posterior buckling of both leaflets (classic pattern)
distribution: middle scallop of posterior leaflet (P2 segment)
discriminating_features: abrupt midsystolic posterior buckling of both leaflets (classic pattern)
LA NOT enlarged (unless associated with significant mitral regurgitation)
interruption of CD line with bulge toward left atrium:
"hammocklike" pansystolic posterior bowing of both leaflets
multiple scallops on mitral valve leaflets (short-axis parasternal view)

general: LA NOT enlarged (unless associated with significant mitral regurgitation)
interruption of CD line with bulge toward left atrium:
abrupt midsystolic posterior buckling of both leaflets (classic pattern)
"hammocklike" pansystolic posterior bowing of both leaflets
multiple scallops on mitral valve leaflets (short-axis parasternal view)
valve leaflets may appear > 5 mm thick (myxomatous degeneration + valve redundancy)
± flail leaflet = eversion of mitral leaflet tip into atrium during systole (← rupture of
mitral valve leaflets passing > 2 mm posterior to plane of mitral annulus (apical 4-chamber
mitral annulus may be dilated > 4.7 cm2
